steps.title: Identify the equation components
steps.content: The equation $3x + 28y = 4,291$ has coefficients 3 and 28 representing the park and residential area in hectares. These multiply with variables to sum to 4,291 total trees.

steps.title: Recognize what the equation structure means
steps.content:
For the equation to equal the total number of trees, each product (area times variable) must represent the trees in that region. Therefore:
$$3x = \text{trees in park}$$
$$28y = \text{trees in residential area}$$

steps.title: Interpret x
steps.content:
Since $3x$ equals the trees in the park and 3 is the park's area in hectares, $x$ must be the number of trees per hectare:
$$x = \frac{\text{trees in park}}{3} = \text{trees per hectare in park}$$
This is the average number of trees per hectare in the park.

That matches Choice A — The average number of trees per hectare in the park.
